2606.14626.title: Characterizing Cultural Localization in AI-Generated Stories
2606.14626.paper_summary: The global use of artificial intelligence has increased interest in assessing the ability to generate culturally localized content, including stories. Cultural localization in stories often occurs through either templated localization -- the use of cultural markers (e.g., names, locations) in a generic narrative -- or holistic localization -- the variation of plots, values, and themes, in addition to cultural markers. We propose a method to measure the degree to which content was generated through templated localization. Specifically, we identify the lexical tokens that distinguish stories across nationalities and measure the similarity of the narratives that remain after removing them. In stories generated by five models on 125 topics for 193 nationalities, our method is able to detect that only a small subset (9-17%) of the vocabulary accounts for the variation across nationalities and that the narratives that remain after removing them contain repeated multi-word sequences, suggesting the presence of a shared culturally-agnostic narrative template. Finally, we characterize the cultural markers for their stereotypicality and offensiveness, finding that markers from 19 countries, mostly located in the Global South, are on average offensive.
2606.14626.paper_summary_zh: 全球對人工智慧的使用增加了評估生成文化本地化內容能力的興趣，包括故事。故事中的文化本地化通常通過模板化本地化進行——在通用敘事中使用文化標記（例如，名字、地點）——或整體本地化進行——情節、價值觀和主題的變化，除了文化標記之外。我們提出了一種方法來測量內容是通過模板化本地化生成的程度。具體而言，我們識別出區分不同國籍故事的詞彙標記，並測量在去除這些標記後剩餘敘事的相似性。在五個模型生成的125個主題和193個國籍的故事中，我們的方法能夠檢測到只有一小部分（9-17%）的詞彙解釋了不同國籍之間的變化，而在去除這些詞彙後剩餘的敘事包含重複的多詞序列，這表明存在一個共享的文化無關敘事模板。最後，我們對文化標記進行了刻板印象和冒犯性的特徵分析，發現來自19個國家的標記，主要位於全球南方，平均上是冒犯性的。
2606.14626.author: Shaily Bhatt et.al.
2606.14626.authors: Shaily Bhatt, Supriti Vijay, Jeremiah Milbauer, Fernando Diaz
